commit.message:
Reprocess sprite sheets and fix all animation quality issues

Jumpshot: reprocessed image from 8x1 (240px-wide) to 4x2 (480px-wide)
by padding each frame with transparent pixels. Character now renders at
95px wide instead of 47px — matching dribble proportions.

Stepback: changed frameHeight 1434→717 since pixel analysis showed all
art ends at exactly y=717 (bottom half is 100% empty). Now uses same
grid as running dribble — consistent scale, origin, and display size.

Frame rates bumped: static dribble 5→8fps, jumpshot 8→12fps, stepback 12→14fps
for smoother animation playback.

Basketball crop: fixed coordinates to (957,632,120,120) based on actual
ball position at pixels (960,635)→(1074,749).

Origins recalculated from pixel-exact art bounds analysis:
- Jumpshot: origin 0.91 (feet at y≈1135 in 1150-tall frame)
- Stepback: origin 0.85 (standard, now 717-tall frames)

feat(#275): add task forecasting to predict future resource usage

Add `anvil task forecast` command that projects future task executions
based on cron schedules with support for:

- Chronological schedule projection with --days and --task filters
- Resource contention detection (--contention) using sweep-line algorithm
- Cost projection (--cost) from historical RunRecord averages
- High-frequency task summarization (>50 runs grouped by day)
- JSON output (--json) for programmatic consumption
- What-if analysis via `anvil add --dry-run` showing forecast impact
- Multi-project support via --all flag

New internal/forecast package with 14 tests covering core engine,
contention detection, and cost statistics.

refactor(arch): promote workspace/ to root level (L1 template, L2 populated)

BREAKING: workspace/ moved from knowledge/workspace/ to root level.
This aligns with the architectural decision that business data is a
first-class citizen alongside knowledge/ and agents/, not nested under it.

- workspace/ now at root: _org, _team, _finance, _meetings, _automations, _tools, inbox/
- core/paths.py: WORKSPACE = ROOT / "workspace" (replaces KNOWLEDGE_WORKSPACE)
- bucket_processor.py: updated imports to use WORKSPACE
- directory-contract.md: updated architecture diagram and directory table
- reference/SOURCE-TREE.md: updated top-level structure
- .gitignore: workspace/ tracked as L1 template, L2 populated

Layer behavior:
  L1: workspace/ structure (template dirs, index.md, setup docs)
  L2: workspace/ populated (real business data)
  L3: knowledge/personal/ remains gitignored

79 tests pass. Ruff clean.

commit.message:
feat: add Phase 1 post-pipeline analysis (4 priorities)

Priority 1: focused_progressive_ablation() — per-dim correlation
distribution + enriched comparison table for 8 MANDATORY targets.

Priority 2: logistic_cv_auc() + probe_binary_variable() — replaces
Ridge R² with AUC-ROC for binary targets (bac_detected fix).

Priority 3: forward_with_resample() + ood_norm_diagnostic() +
resample_ablation() — OOD-robust ablation (Grant et al. 2025) with
L2 norm diagnostic comparing mean-clamp vs resample vs random.

Priority 4: cross-circuit summary table (hippocampal vs L5PC).

New orchestration: scripts/run_phase1_analysis.py runs all 4.

commit.message:
refactor: harden MCP server with config validation, transaction safety, and circuit breaker (#27)

Implements 10-fix MCP hardening plan plus infrastructure cleanup:
- Config validation: Replace z.any() with proper Zod schema (ConversationTestSpecSchema, RedTeamAttackSchema, PlatformConfigSchema)
- Callback safety: Wrap DELETE+INSERT+UPDATE in transaction to prevent race conditions
- Stuck run cleanup: Add 10min TTL for remote queued runs, 5min for relay, 1hr for running
- Long-poll timeout: Reduce to 45s to avoid MCP client timeouts
- Circuit breaker: Abort after 3 consecutive connection failures, reset on non-connection errors
- Pre-flight health check: Single diagnostic result instead of N identical failures
- Structured relay response: JSON with run_id, status, relay_command fields
- Configurable agent port: Support agent_port config field (default 3001)
- Worker repeat count: Use repeat-aware sum instead of length
- Remove dead infrastructure code: Clean up audio_tests and infrastructure from types, schemas, dashboard

Dashboard UI updates: Remove infrastructure tab, probe scenarios, audio_tests config section. Preserve backward compatibility for historical data via string casts.

Co-authored-by: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix site report PDF layout — use floats instead of CSS grid

PrinceXML (DocRaptor's engine) doesn't support CSS grid or aspect-ratio,
causing photos to stack 1 per page instead of 2 columns. Switch to
float-based layout with explicit height for reliable 2-column rendering.

commit.message:
Bind n8n to localhost only, add SSH tunnel instructions

n8n's secure cookie blocks HTTP access via LAN IP. Instead of
disabling security, bind n8n to 127.0.0.1 only and guide users
to use an SSH tunnel (ssh -L 5678:localhost:5678 jetson). This is
more secure — port 5678 is no longer exposed on the network, and
access requires SSH key authentication.

commit.message:
feat: v5 refactor — intelligence terminal architecture

Complete rewrite of Macro Intel from news aggregator to live intelligence platform:

- New event data model with 12 regions, 12 categories, 3 severity tiers, 4 source tiers
- 22 active feeds (9 Tier 1 official + 13 Tier 2 reputable) replacing 8 RSS feeds
- Event clustering/deduplication engine (Union-Find with Jaccard similarity)
- Proper geolocation (80+ countries, 25+ institutions, 18 waterways, no fake coords)
- IndexedDB archive (Dexie.js) with full search, filters, pagination, event log
- Plain English summaries replacing jargon-heavy brief generator
- Severity-based output: Routine/Material/Major with different detail levels
- React Router (HashRouter) with 5 pages: Dashboard, Events, Map, Markets, Archive
- 2D Leaflet map replacing 3D globe (958KB bundle vs 2,561KB)
- Source lineage preserved on every event with tier classification

commit.message:
feat: add real JWT auth, PostgreSQL database, and push notifications

Auth System:
- Real email/password login with bcrypt password hashing
- JWT token-based authentication (7-day expiry)
- Auth middleware protects all API routes
- Token persisted in localStorage with auto-restore on refresh
- Proper error handling for invalid credentials
- Loading spinner during auth check and login

Database Migration (SQLite → PostgreSQL):
- Neon serverless PostgreSQL replaces in-memory SQLite
- New schema: users, sessions, attendance, push_subscriptions, notifications
- Seed script with 9 demo users (1 admin, 2 coaches, 6 players)
- 3 seeded training sessions across groups G1, G2, G3

Push Notifications:
- VAPID key generation and web-push integration
- Service worker handles push events and notification clicks
- Per-user subscription storage (supports multiple devices)
- Admin can send targeted notifications (by user, group, or broadcast)
- /api/push/subscribe, /api/push/send, /api/push/vapid-key endpoints

Demo Credentials:
- admin@wolves.jo / Admin123!
- coach.sam@wolves.jo / Coach123!
- ahmad@wolves.jo / Player123!

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

evolve(motes): hardy shape + wanderer lean — temperament silhouettes

All three temperaments now have distinct visual identities:
- Hardy motes (hardiness > 0.55): wide squared shoulders from filled upper
  corners give a blocky, resilient silhouette. Silvery-blue corner sparks
  fire when standing on hostile terrain (shield-flash: resisting, not dying).
- Wanderers (wanderlust > 0.65, moving fast): head/neck pixels lean 1px
  into movement direction — a running posture visible within seconds.
- Social motes: resonance ring (already present) completes the trio.
